notes: Keep playbooks abstract and short (5-7 nodes). Don't encode protocol specifics — let the planner instantiate against the user's actual subject at runtime. Grow only when a real prompt fails because no playbook fit.

playbooks.name: Evaluate a protocol
playbooks.description: General-purpose deep dive on a single protocol. Works for L1s, DEXes, lending markets, perp DEXes, LSTs. Don't specialize unless v0 fails.
playbooks.trigger_pattern: User asks to 'evaluate', 'deep dive on', 'build a thesis on', 'tell me about' a single protocol or token.

nodes.abstract: Where is the protocol today? Headline KPIs.
Is it growing? What's the trend?
How does the token actually accrue value?
Who else is in this market and where does it rank?
What is it worth under reasonable scenarios?
Pull it together into a one-paragraph view.

playbooks.name: Compare a set of protocols
playbooks.description: Side-by-side analysis across N protocols. Triggered when the prompt names multiple subjects or asks 'best X'.
playbooks.trigger_pattern: User names 2+ protocols/tokens or asks 'best/top N in {category}'.

nodes.abstract: What dimensions matter for this comparison?
Pull the headline metrics for each subject.
Where does each one differ structurally (tokenomics, mechanism)?
Render the comparison table with takeaway per row.
Order them and explain the top pick.

playbooks.name: Build a scenario / valuation model
playbooks.description: User explicitly asks for a price target, model, or scenario analysis on a single asset.
playbooks.trigger_pattern: User asks 'what's X worth at Y', 'build a model for', 'price target', 'scenario analysis', 'NTM revenue'.
playbooks.source_analyst: TopherGMI valuation; thiccyth0t for derivatives revenue.

nodes.abstract: What are the explicit drivers that move the metric forward?
What multiples do comparables trade at? Anchor the multiple range.
Bear / base / bull on the driver; apply multiple range; produce a 3-scenario table.
Which input matters most? Flag the assumption with the highest leverage.
